text: Working with APIs is a common task for modern developers. When you make an HTTP request to an API, you usually send or receive data in JSON format, which is easy for both humans and machines to read. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to ensure only authorised users can access the service. It is also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, good error handling is essential—always check the response status codes and handle failures gracefully to make your application more reliable.
